events.title: Bison-tennial Community Exhibition Opening
events.description: Join us on Thursday, May 28, from 5 to 7 p.m. for the community exhibition opening of Bison-tennial: From Colonization to Rematriation . This is a FREE community event. Bison-tennial explores the profound influence of bison on the history, cultures, and landscapes of the North American West. The exhibition traces their story from pre-colonial times through westward expansion and the near-destruction of the great herds—arriving at today’s growing restoration and rematriation efforts. During the opening event at the Museum, explore our new summer exhibitions, participate in an exciting scaven...
events.location: Jackson, WY

events.title: Jackson Hole Birding Festival: Bird Wild!
events.description: Join Wild Excellence Films for Jackson Hole Birding Festival presentations at The Center Theater! Jackson Hole Birding Festival The Jackson Hole Birding Festival returns to Jackson, Wyoming, this spring for its second year, offering birders from across the country an immersive four-day celebration of birds, conservation, and the spectacular landscapes of the Greater Yellowstone Ecosystem. The festival runs May 27–30, 2026, and opening night festivities on May 27 will be held at the Center for the Arts. events.title: River in Reverse
events.description: Artists Terry Chambers, Bland Hoke, Jr., and Shane Lindsey. “River in Reverse” depicts 16 miles of the Snake River, from Dead Man’s Bar to the Jackson Hole Airport. The work is backlit at night with color-changing LED lights to reflect the changing color of the river throughout the seasons. “River in Reverse” is a byproduct of a commission for the Jackson Hole Airport, using the scrap metal that would have otherwise been recycled. Installed in September 2011, “River in Reverse” wraps around The Center’s ceramics studio along the highly-trafficked pathway connecting Glenwood and Cache. The s...

events.title: Patrick Dougherty: Finders Keepers
events.description: Patrick Dougherty is an American environmental artist, best known for his sculptures and installations built with sticks and saplings. Finders Keepers, a site-specific sculpture, was constructed over the course of three weeks with the help of many local volunteers. It is made entirely out of locally sourced saplings found in Teton and Sublette Counties. This sculpture is temporary and we hope all ages will enjoy it throughout the seasons for as long as possible. For your safety, and to help ensure others can enjoy this artwork over time, please do not climb on the sculpture. You are welcome...
events.location: Center for the Arts, 240 S Glenwood St, Jackson, WY, 83001
events.source: Center for the Arts Jackson Hole

events.title: Chess Club
events.description: Join local chess players for a fun and friendly evening, each Tuesday in The Center Commons. Whether you’re a beginner or a seasoned player, all ages and skill levels are welcome. Meet fellow chess enthusiasts, learn new strategies, and enjoy a relaxed, inclusive environment. Some boards and pieces are provided, but you are encouraged to bring your own, as well. Snacks and non-alcoholic drinks are welcome.

events.title: Music Classes
events.description: Center for the Arts is proud to host two invaluable music education nonprofits on its campus. MusicLand and Teton Music School offer afterschool, adult and summer camp programming, with instruction ranging from intensive music classes to guitar and piano lessons in Jackson, Wyoming. MusicLand MusicLand offers a comprehensive range of music education programs for children of all ages. events.title: Visual Arts Classes
events.description: As a resident organization on The Center’s campus, the Art Association of Jackson Hole (A!A) hosts programming across five studios, an art supply store, and an art gallery. A!A provides over 10,000 square-feet of space for community members to learn, practice and exhibit art. Offering Jackson Hole art classes and workshops to adults and kids of all levels, A!A instructs on painting, drawing, ceramics, sculpture, photography, silversmithing, glass, printmaking, mixed media, and more. It also hosts outreach sessions for more than 30 service organizations in the valley, providing underserved g...
events.location: Center for the Arts, 240 S Glenwood St, Jackson, WY, 83001
events.source: Center for the Arts Jackson Hole

events.title: Writing Classes and Workshops
events.description: Jackson Hole Writers host workshops and craft intensives throughout the year both in-person and online on The Center’s Campus. Jackson Hole Writers is a community of writers, building connections and creating educational experiences for established and aspiring writers across genres. Jackson Hole Writers envisions a world where all writers are empowered to achieve personal success through opportunity and support. To learn more about Jackson Hole Writers visit their website for a full list of programming for children and adults.
events.location: Center for the Arts, 240 S Glenwood St, Jackson, WY, 83001
events.source: Center for the Arts Jackson Hole

events.title: Dance and Movement Classes
events.description: As a resident organization at The Center, Dancers’ Workshop (DW) offers dance and movement classes for children and adults of all ages in Jackson, Wyoming. In addition to its year-round programming, DW fundraises annually to bring world-renowned dance talent like the San Francisco Ballet and the Bill T. Jones/Arnie Zane Company to The Center’s stage. For more information on weekly classes – including Jackson Hole dance lessons for kids and adults – as well as other programming, please visit the Dancers’ Workshop website.
events.location: Center for the Arts, 240 S Glenwood St, Jackson, WY, 83001
events.source: Center for the Arts Jackson Hole

events.title: Youth Art Programming
events.description: The Art Association of Jackson Hole’s youth art programming is all about creativity, color, and fun! Kids of all ages and interests will dive into exciting projects that spark imagination and bring out the artist in everyone! With after-school classes offered Monday through Thursday, and week-long summer day camps, A!A’s programs make it easy to fit creativity into your family’s schedule! events.title: Adult Art Programming
events.description: Join the Art Association for Adult Art Classes on The Center Campus! The Art Association of Jackson Hole’s adult classes and workshops are in a wide range of mediums including ceramics classes, painting classes and workshops, drawing classes, printmaking, digital arts, photography, and silversmithing for artists of all abilities. A!A offers a variety of programming, including Try-Nights, multi-week classes and intensive workshops. Past Visiting Artist workshops have included Jeremy Morgan, Stacy Phillips, and Elder Jones. events.title: Bordando Esperanza – Embroidering Hope
events.description: Bordados devocionales – devotional embroideries, hand-stitched prayers and memories by asylum-seekers stranded at the U.S.-Mexico Border from 2019 – 2022, tell the story of family migration and displacement through the hands and eyes of those living it. Each original bordado or manta is a testimony of the faith that sustains the maker and the hope they hold close against all odds. Artisans Beyond Borders, a bi-national migration ministry of Tucson’s Grace St. Paul’s Episcopal Church, invites you join the national tour of the exhibition Bordando Esperanza ~ Embroidering Hope: Retablos of Asy...

events.title: Ephraim Heller
events.description: About the Artist Ephraim Heller began photographing nature and his family as a 10-year-old child in rural Massachusetts, where he ran barefoot in the forest and swam in ponds with turtles and snakes. Heller was fascinated by old mechanical cameras, which he took apart but could never reassemble. One day, Heller attempted underwater photography in a backyard pond by putting his camera in a plastic bag. It didn’t end well. Another time, his father loaned him his camera, which was a relatively expensive item for their family. As Heller rode his bicycle, he broke off the lens in the spokes. For...
events.location: Center for the Arts, 240 S Glenwood St, Jackson, WY, 83001
events.source: Center for the Arts Jackson Hole

events.title: Inverted Florarium by Bronwyn Minton
events.description: Inverted Florarium is an art installation at Center for the Arts that explores ecology, resilience, and perspective through softness, inversion, and the playful reimagining of a natural form. A large bloom, inspired by Wyoming’s state flower – the Indian Paintbrush (Castilleja spp.) — hovers, upside down, above a field of colorful blossoms. The flower’s tendrils connect and tether the shapes below. The surrounding glass courtyard functions as a terrarium, framing the installation as both ecosystem and specimen. Minton draws on the tradition of soft sculpture, while infusing the work with an...

events.title: Fables, Feathers & Fur
events.description: Fun for ages 3-6 Storytelling and art-making at the museum is an exciting opportunity for young visitors to engage with the art through looking, reading, and art-making in the galleries. All materials are provided. Walk-ins welcome! Registration is only required for groups of 10 or more. We can also accommodate educators interested in scheduling Fables, Feathers & Fur programming for their respective groups. For more information and to schedule your group, please contact swilson@wildlifeart.org . Generously sponsored by Nancy & Dick Collister.
events.location: Jackson, WY

events.title: Sneak Peek of Art in Motion: Kinetic Sculpture with Ivan Iler
events.description: FREE to Museum members and included in the cost of general admission. Join us for a sneak peek of Art in Motion: Kinetic Sculpture with Ivan Iler, with Sugden Chief Curator of Education Kelly Skeen Fried, who will share insights into the exhibition and introduce special guest Thomas Hill, one of the participating artists. Hill, born in the United Kingdom and now based in San Francisco, CA, creates work that explores physical movement and his interactions with the natural world. Sneak Peek attendees receive 10% off in Palate restaurant when dining for lunch after the event!

events.description: Join Jackson Hole Public Art for Your Brain on Art at The Center Theater! Your Brain on Art Join us for an inspiring and thought‑provoking evening with nationally acclaimed authors Susan Magsamen and Ivy Ross as they present “Your Brain on Art: How the Arts Transform Us,” a groundbreaking exploration of the science behind the transformative power of the arts. Your Brain on Art features Susan Magsamen and Ivy Ross as they share groundbreaking research on the biological impact of creativity. Walk-Up Wednesday tours are included in your membership or the cost of your admission ticket for that day. If you would like to join us on a tour, please meet in Sullivan Hall by our Walk-Up Wednesdays sign just before 11 a.m. The tour will be 45 minutes long and is filled on a first-come, first served basis.

The band will perform their classic album in its entirety, in sequence. Originally released on May 21, 1996, Bringing Down the Horse was a critical and commercial triumph. events.title: Yoga on the Trail
events.description: Thursdays in July & August Top Jackson Hole yoga instructors conduct weekly summer classes outside on the south part of the Sculpture Trail (parking lot to your left as you come up the hill). Participants will feel inspired doing Sun Salutations and Downward Dogs as they look across the grand vista of the National Elk Refuge. Join us in connecting with two art forms—visual art inspired by artists over the centuries, and the art of yoga, ensuring healthy mind, body, and spirit. All levels welcome. Free. *Please bring your own mat and water!

events.title: Classical Music Listening Club at Teton County Library
events.description: The Concerto and the Symphony: We know that most orchestra concerts have a concerto on the first half and a symphony on the second half – but what exactly do those terms mean, and how have they evolved throughout music history? We’ll use two beloved works from GTMF’s opening classical concert – Rachmaninoff’s Piano Concerto No. 2 and Tchaikovsky’s Symphony No. 5 – to understand the key concepts behind the two most common forms in the orchestral repertoire, as well as compare them to examples of earlier and later styles. Dive into classical music with the Grand Teton Music Festival! Suitable...

events.title: On the Road: Brass Under Blues Skies at Murie Ranch
events.description: Charlie Daval, trumpet Justin Kohan, trumpet Julie Beckel, horn Jay Evans, trombone John DiCesare, tuba Brass Under Blues Skies: Revel in the majestic sounds of the GTMF Brass Quintet while the sun sets behind the mountains at the Murie Ranch. This program will feature classical favorites, nostalgic jazz, and music that celebrates the great outdoors. In GTMF’s On the Road series, Stoner Family Education Curator Meaghan Heinrich leads programs that are accessible to all ages and bring music to life with explanations and demonstrations. Programming for this free concert is forthcoming.

Join us in connecting with two art forms—visual art inspired by artists over the centuries, and the art of yoga, ensuring healthy mind, body, and spirit. All levels welcome. Free. *Please bring your own mat and water!

events.description: Thursdays in July & August Top Jackson Hole yoga instructors conduct weekly summer classes outside on the south part of the Sculpture Trail (parking lot to your left as you come up the hill). Participants will feel inspired doing Sun Salutations and Downward Dogs as they look across the grand vista of the National Elk Refuge. Join us in connecting with two art forms—visual art inspired by artists over the centuries, and the art of yoga, ensuring healthy mind, body, and spirit. All levels welcome. Free. *Please bring your own mat and water!

events.description: Beloved Festival Orchestra member Conrad Jones moves from the brass section to the front of the stage to perform Haydn’s Trumpet Concerto, one of the finest concertos ever written for the instrument, and the only one Haydn ever wrote. In contrast to Haydn’s singular piece are two works by important 20th century icons – a playful literary overture by American Samuel Barber, The School for Scandal Overture, and an incredibly precocious college graduation project by Dmitri Shostakovich, his Symphony No. 1. program Barber: The School for Scandal Overture Haydn: Trumpet Concerto Shostakovich: Sy...

events.title: Classical Music Listening Club at Teton County Library
events.description: Youthful Spirit and Mature Mastery: Samuel Barber’s The School for Scandal Overture and Shostakovich’s Symphony No. 1 were both composed while their respective composers were still in college, while Haydn penned his Trumpet Concerto at the age of 64. How does the age and life experience of composers shape the sound of their music? In this session, we’ll look at works written in both the early and late stages of composers’ careers… and maybe a few midlife crisis pieces too! Dive into classical music with the Grand Teton Music Festival! Suitable for interested novices and seasoned fans alike,...

events.description: Join artist Hayley Ruland for a fun and creative Paint By Glass workshop! In this hands-on class, participants will create their own wildlife-inspired landscape featuring a bison using mosaic glass and wood. You’ll bring your piece to life by selecting from a variety of glass colors to design your unique landscape. You’ll learn how to nip and glue glass into the custom 3D wooden palette to create a unique mixed-media artwork full of color and texture. events.title: Classical Music Listening Club at Teton County Library
events.description: Timbre through Time: Cowbells, coconut shells, a duck call and a referee’s whistle – these are just a few instruments called for in James MacMillan’s Britannia, composed in 1994. But why does the orchestra shrink so much for the romantic-era violin concerto of Felix Mendelssohn and classical-era Symphony No. 104 of Haydn? The art of orchestration is all about which instruments play and when. We’ll learn about the evolution of the orchestra’s instruments and how and why it has expanded to include so many new colors. Dive into classical music with the Grand Teton Music Festival! Suitable for ...

events.description: Enjoy a behind-the-scenes look as Sir Donald Runnicles and the orchestra rehearse for the weekend Festival Orchestra concerts. events.title: Classical Music Listening Club at Teton County Library
events.description: Cinematic Soundscapes: Whether crafted for the silver screen or the concert hall, music is a powerful way to take us to imaginative new places by giving us a soundtrack. We’ll study Beethoven’s Pastoral Symphony to see how a composer put the sounds of nature into his music, then see how golden age film composer Erich Korngold brought the sound of the movies to his violin concerto. Finally, we’ll see how these pieces influenced modern film scores, including John Barry’s music for Out of Africa and Dances with Wolves. Dive into classical music with the Grand Teton Music Festival! Suitable for...
events.location: Teton County Library in Jackson

events.title: On the Road: Up Close and Personal at Murie Ranch
events.description: Eva Cappelletti Chao, violin Phillipe Chao, viola Up Close and Personal: Partners in music and life, Phillipe Chao and Eva Cappelletti Chao, present a program of violin and viola duos interwoven with stories from their many years playing together at the Grand Teton Music Festival – a fitting tribute to the spirit of Olaus and Mardie Murie’s home and legacy. In GTMF’s On the Road series, Stoner Family Education Curator Meaghan Heinrich leads programs that are accessible to all ages and bring music to life with explanations and demonstrations. Programming for this free concert is forthcoming.

events.title: Little Feat
events.description: Little Feat Playing is the joy and satisfaction of touring, but it comes with the hard part—travel, the endless miles on a bus. There’s no dodging the wear and tear, and Little Feat has been playing for quite a while. With tongue stuck somewhat in cheek, Little Feat announces “The Last Farewell Tour,” which will begin in April 2026. It’s definitely not an absolute, never-gonna-play again statement. events.title: Yoga on the Trail
events.description: Thursdays in July & August Top Jackson Hole yoga instructors conduct weekly summer classes outside on the south part of the Sculpture Trail (parking lot to your left as you come up the hill). Participants will feel inspired doing Sun Salutations and Downward Dogs as they look across the grand vista of the National Elk Refuge. Join us in connecting with two art forms—visual art inspired by artists over the centuries, and the art of yoga, ensuring healthy mind, body, and spirit. All levels welcome. Free. *Please bring your own mat and water!

events.title: Classical Music Listening Club at Teton County Library
events.description: Italian Inspiration: A young Felix Mendelssohn composed his “Italian” Symphony after returning from a 10-month trip to the country, while native Italian Giacomo Puccini became the most well-known voice in opera at the turn of the century. How did a visiting composer and one who spent most of his life and career there use different approaches to bring the sounds of a culture to life? We’ll look at Mendelssohn’s Symphony No. 4 and Puccini’s arias, as well as other music inspired by both foreign travels and devotion to homeland. events.title: Big Thief
events.description: Big Thief Big Thief comes to The Center Theater with their “Somersault Slide 360 Tour.” Over the last decade, Big Thief has proven themselves to be one of the most exciting live bands of their generation. Known for classics from their debut album “Masterpiece” like “Velvet Ring” and “Paul,” Big Thief stands out in the indie folk-rock scene with their emotionally-resonant lyrics and sound. Composed of songwriter Adrianne Lenker, guitarist Buck Meek, and drummer James Krivchenia, their creative output is seemingly limitless, sprawling across five critically-acclaimed albums. events.title: Rise of the Western Lights: Mark Maggiori
events.description: This September, Jackson Hole Art Auction, in partnership with Far West, will present a rare solo exhibition by celebrated Western painter, Mark Maggiori Opening during the Jackson Hole Fall Arts Festival and alongside JHAA’s 20th Annual Live Auction, the exhibition marks Maggiori’s first solo presentation in three years. The show will unveil 20–25 works for auction, ballot sale & showcase. Friday, September 18 10AM MDT – Artist Talk with Mark Maggiori 6PM MDT – Auction of the works at the Center for the Arts auditorium. Live stream and online bidding available via jacksonholeartauction.com ...

events.title: 39th Western Visions® Evening Sale
events.description: Western Visions ® is the National Museum of Wildlife Art’s largest and longest-running fundraiser and is one of the signature events of the Jackson Hole Fall Arts Festival. Top contemporary wildlife artists and collectors from around the world will come together at the Museum in Jackson Hole, Wyoming. The show features a wide selection of art for sale and supports participating artists and all we do here at the Museum.
